diagrams.title: Trigger Execution Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the complete sequence when a DML statement triggers multiple triggers on a table
elements: User executes DML (UPDATE emp SET sal = sal * 1.1)
BEFORE EACH ROW trigger fires (for each affected row)
AFTER EACH ROW trigger fires (for each affected row)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the timing and sequence of trigger execution

diagrams.title: Assertion vs Trigger vs Check Constraint
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing where each constraint type can be applied
elements: CHECK Constraint: Table-level, Single table, Declarative
Trigger: Table-level, Can perform actions, Procedural
diagrams.purpose: Choosing the right constraint type for different scenarios

diagrams.title: Row-Level vs Statement-Level Trigger
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of how each trigger type fires based on number of affected rows
elements: Row-Level: Fires 5 times (once per row) - uses OLD/NEW
Statement-Level: Fires 1 time (for entire statement) - no row access
diagrams.purpose: Understanding trigger granularity and when to use each type
